title: dryopteris aneitensis
intro: Dryopteris aneitensis, also known as the Tahitian shield fern, is a species of fern native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Tahiti. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, and it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Dryopteris aneitensis typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically in the form of creams or ointments.
